themes.title: Trump’s White House Correspondents’ Dinner shooting dominates global right-wing coverage
themes.blurb: The attempted assassination of Donald Trump at the White House Correspondents' Dinner has become a dominant transnational narrative, with right-wing media in multiple countries framing it as evidence of rising political violence and left-wing extremism. Leaders like Giorgia Meloni and global far-right figures have condemned the attack, reinforcing solidarity with Trump and his administration.

countries.name: 🇺🇸 MAGA / Trump
countries.note: Intense coverage of Trump’s evacuation, suspect’s motives, and security failures; used to rally base and criticize media.

countries.name: 🇮🇹 Fratelli d'Italia / Lega
countries.note: Meloni condemned the attack, linking it to broader concerns about political violence and democratic erosion.

countries.name: 🇫🇷 Rassemblement National
countries.note: Jordan Bardella expressed solidarity with Trump, framing the incident as part of a global struggle against left-wing extremism.

countries.name: 🇬🇧 Reform UK
countries.note: Nigel Farage used the event to criticize U.S. security and media, aligning with Trump’s narrative of victimization.

countries.name: 🇪🇸 Vox
countries.note: Abascal condemned the attack, tying it to Spain’s own political tensions and advocating for stronger security measures.

countries.name: 🇩🇪 Alternative für Deutschland
countries.note: AfD framed the incident as evidence of systemic failures in Western democracies, calling for stronger leadership.

countries.name: 🇵🇱 Prawo i Sprawiedliwość
countries.note: PiS condemned the attack, using it to criticize U.S. political polarization and highlight Trump’s resilience.

themes.title: Far-right parties surge in European polls
themes.blurb: Right-wing and far-right parties across Europe are gaining momentum in polls, with Germany’s AfD leading national surveys and similar trends in France, Spain, and Scandinavia. This theme reflects parallel electoral successes and shared strategies to capitalize on immigration, economic anxiety, and Euroscepticism.

countries.name: 🇩🇪 Alternative für Deutschland
countries.note: AfD leads German polls at 28%, capitalizing on immigration and economic concerns.

countries.name: 🇫🇷 Rassemblement National
countries.note: RN prepares for 2027 elections, focusing on immigration and economic policies to consolidate support.

countries.name: 🇪🇸 Vox
countries.note: Vox gains influence through regional pacts with PP, pushing anti-immigration and nationalist policies.

countries.name: 🇸🇪 Sverigedemokraterna
countries.note: SD blocks expanded congestion tax in Gothenburg, emphasizing rural investment and anti-immigration policies.

countries.name: 🇳🇴 Fremskrittspartiet
countries.note: FrP opposes social media bans for minors, framing it as an attack on freedom of expression.

countries.name: 🇬🇧 Reform UK
countries.note: Reform UK gains ground with anti-immigration policies, targeting Muslim communities and pushing for mass deportations.

themes.title: PP-Vox coalitions spark constitutional debates
themes.blurb: The formation of coalition governments between Spain’s Partido Popular (PP) and Vox in regions like Extremadura and Aragón has ignited cross-national debates about constitutional legality, discrimination, and the normalization of far-right policies. Left-wing parties and media frame these pacts as threats to democratic values, while right-wing outlets defend them as pragmatic governance.

countries.name: 🇪🇸 Vox
countries.note: Vox’s 'national priority' policy in regional pacts with PP sparks legal and ethical debates, with left-wing parties accusing it of discrimination.

countries.name: 🇪🇸 Partido Popular
countries.note: PP’s alliances with Vox in Extremadura and Aragón face internal dissent and external criticism for adopting far-right policies.

countries.name: 🇫🇷 Rassemblement National
countries.note: RN’s policies on immigration and national identity are compared to Vox’s 'national priority' stance, highlighting shared far-right strategies.

countries.name: 🇮🇹 Fratelli d'Italia / Lega
countries.note: Meloni’s government faces similar debates over immigration and EU fiscal rules, with Lega pushing for unilateral exits from stability pacts.

themes.title: Orbán’s resignation reshapes Hungarian politics
themes.blurb: Viktor Orbán’s decision to step down from Hungary’s parliament following his party’s election defeat has triggered a cross-national ripple effect, with right-wing media in Poland, Germany, and beyond analyzing the implications for Hungary’s future and the broader European far-right. The rise of Péter Magyar’s Tisza Party is framed as a turning point for Hungary’s political landscape.

countries.name: 🇭🇺 Fidesz / Tisza
countries.note: Orbán’s resignation and the rise of Péter Magyar’s Tisza Party dominate coverage, with debates over Hungary’s future and EU relations.

countries.name: 🇵🇱 Prawo i Sprawiedliwość
countries.note: PiS analyzes Orbán’s downfall, drawing parallels to Poland’s political dynamics and potential leadership shifts.

countries.name: 🇩🇪 Alternative für Deutschland
countries.note: AfD comments on Orbán’s resignation, framing it as a cautionary tale for Europe’s far-right movements.

countries.name: 🇪🇺 EU Relations
countries.note: EU media and officials discuss Hungary’s political shift, focusing on the potential unlocking of frozen EU funds and rule-of-law reforms.

themes.title: Far-right rhetoric fuels political violence
themes.blurb: Right-wing and far-right media in Italy, Spain, the UK, and the U.S. are amplifying incidents of political violence, often blaming left-wing groups or migrants. This theme reflects a shared narrative that frames far-right policies as necessary responses to perceived threats, while downplaying violence linked to their own supporters.

countries.name: 🇮🇹 Fratelli d'Italia / Lega
countries.note: Meloni’s government faces criticism for downplaying violence at Liberation Day events, with far-right rhetoric blamed for escalating tensions.

countries.name: 🇪🇸 Vox
countries.note: Vox’s anti-immigration rhetoric is linked to protests and violence, with left-wing groups accusing the party of inciting hatred.

countries.name: 🇬🇧 Reform UK
countries.note: Farage’s rhetoric is tied to protests and clashes, with critics accusing Reform UK of fueling division.

countries.name: 🇺🇸 MAGA / Trump
countries.note: Trump’s rhetoric is blamed for inspiring political violence, with media amplifying incidents like the WHCD shooting as evidence of rising extremism.

countries.name: 🇫🇷 Rassemblement National
countries.note: RN’s rhetoric on immigration and national identity is criticized for contributing to social tensions and violence.
